title: Contracts as source of truth + shared walkable provenance
summary: The skill-architecture contracts have drifted: seed-example.json documents a base_seed field the seed-schema lacks while setting additionalProperties:false, so Mode-2 (handoff-merge) seeds fail validation across the 11 skills that call validate-schema.sh. Separately, task-schema forces a required prd field, but task files are produced by five skills from non-PRD origins (seeds, debug sessions, code-review, component work), so provenance is lost when prd is set null. Resolution: make contracts/ the single source of truth (skill-local files are pure instances or pointer stubs only), define one shared, walkable provenance concept reused by every lineage contract via cross-file $ref, and replace task's prd with that provenance. Both schemas get one embedded coherence example instead of separate example files. Hard cutover, no migration.
decisions: contracts/ is the single source of truth. Every skill-local schema artifact is demoted to either a pure worked instance that must validate clean against its contract, or a pointer stub that redirects to the contract. No skill-local file may redefine shape, required fields, or field descriptions.

Delete skills/to-seed/resources/seed-example.json. Move one worked, internally coherent example into seed-schema.json's native top-level 'examples' keyword. One file holds shape, descriptions, and example, so there is no cross-file copy to drift.

Provenance is captured by two fields, not one: 'producer' (which skill wrote the file) and 'source' (what the file was derived from). Splitting them keeps each axis clean and lets the chain be reverse-traced.

Provenance is defined ONCE in a new contracts/provenance-schema.json holding $defs.producer and $defs.source. seed-schema and task-schema reference it via cross-file $ref (e.g. {"$ref": "provenance-schema#/$defs/source"}). This is the only physical mechanism that honors 'define once'; embedded copies or build-time inlining were rejected.

'producer' is a closed enum seeded with every lineage-producing skill plus 'manual' (the escape hatch for hand-authored files). 'source.kind' is a closed enum ['seed','prd','session'] with 'task' deferred until a real producer derives from a task file.

Both provenance fields are enforced enums (not open strings) for consistency and typo protection. The maintenance cost is absorbed by codifying enum upkeep as a step in register-skill: a new lineage producer appends its name to the producer enum.

'source' is a nullable object {kind, ref}. source:null means the file is the root of its lineage chain. ref is a path/URL string or null. A reverse-trace tool switches on source.kind to know how to follow source.ref upward.

Retire the seed 'base_seed' field. Mode-2 (handoff-merge) seeds now express lineage through source = {kind:'seed', ref:'<parent seed path>'}. One lineage mechanism for the whole pipeline instead of two.

Reconcile the seed naming collision: seed-schema's current 'source' field (a producer string like 'to-seed') is renamed to 'producer', and the new 'source' object is added. seed and task now share identical provenance vocabulary.
task-schema drops the required 'prd' field and adds required 'producer' + 'source'. PRD becomes just one possible source.kind among several, not an enforced default.

task-schema also gets exactly one embedded coherence example (a to-tasks-from-seed file with a HITL+AFK mix, real blocked_by chains, one browser_verify, one follow_up whose trigger_task references a real task id). Not one-per-producer: task file structure does not vary by producer, only the provenance fields do.
validate-schema.sh gets a one-time upgrade: load every contracts/*.json into a referencing.Registry (Python jsonschema) so cross-file $ref by bare $id resolves for all 11 calling skills.

validate-schema.sh also gains a meta-check that extracts examples[*] from a schema and validates each against that same schema, so the in-file coherence examples cannot drift from their own properties.
validate-schema.sh emits a clear schema_version-mismatch error pointing the user to regenerate via /to-seed, instead of a cryptic property error.

Hard cutover, no migration tooling. schema_version is a const: seed bumps 2 -> 3, task bumps 1 -> 2. Old on-disk files fail validation by design; clean-scaffolding wipes disposable docs and nothing is in flight.

Consumer to-e2e-tasks repoints from the old task 'prd' field to 'source.ref': when ref is non-null (kind seed|prd) it loads that artifact for context; when kind is 'session' (ref null) it falls through to the existing prompt-or-skip path.

Consumer tasks-to-linear derives the Linear title prefix from the task file's own filename slug, not from prd. The task file always has a slug; source.ref can be null. The prefix identifies the task batch, not an upstream document.
Delete the stray resources/task-schema.md and repoint to-tasks (line 75) at contracts/task-schema.json. It violates the source-of-truth rule and is a second task schema doc.
open_threads: Verify the installed Python jsonschema version before wiring cross-file $ref into validate-schema.sh: >=4.18 uses the referencing.Registry API, older versions need the deprecated RefResolver. The design is unchanged either way; only the resolver implementation differs.

Decide whether to-prd-html is a 'producer' in its own right, or whether HTML PRDs carry provenance only through the seed JSON they embed verbatim (in which case to-prd-html does not stamp its own producer/source).

Decide whether to enforce the kind/ref correlation in-schema via if/then (kind='session' requires ref=null; kind in ['seed','prd'] requires a non-null string ref), or leave it to prose convention to avoid gold-plating.
next_action: Implement the contracts changes: create contracts/provenance-schema.json, then update seed-schema (v3) and task-schema (v2) to $ref it, embed examples, and reconcile producer/source.
problem_statement: Skill-architecture contracts allow shape definitions to live in more than one place, and they have already drifted. seed-example.json declares a base_seed field that seed-schema.json lacks under additionalProperties:false, so Mode-2 seeds fail validation. task-schema forces a required 'prd' field even though five producer skills generate task files from non-PRD origins, collapsing all non-PRD provenance to prd:null and making it impossible to tell where a task file came from or to reverse-trace its lineage.
solution: Establish contracts/ as the single source of truth (skill-local files are pure instances or pointer stubs only). Define one shared provenance concept (producer + source) in contracts/provenance-schema.json, referenced by both seed-schema and task-schema via cross-file $ref, with the validator upgraded to resolve refs through a registry. Replace task's prd with provenance, retire seed's base_seed into source, embed one coherence example per schema, and hard-cutover both schema versions.
evidence: An investigation of the contracts and the 11 skills that call validate-schema.sh confirmed: seed-schema has additionalProperties:false and no base_seed property while seed-example.json's _schema block documents base_seed (a live Mode-2 validation break); a deprecated prd-data-schema.json already demonstrates the pointer-stub precedent; a second stray task schema doc (resources/task-schema.md) exists outside contracts/; and the validator is Python jsonschema invoked with a single schema and no ref resolver.
implementation_decisions: New file contracts/provenance-schema.json with $id 'provenance-schema', exposing $defs.producer (closed enum string) and $defs.source (oneOf: {kind enum [seed,prd,session], ref string|null} or null).
seed-schema.json (v3): rename 'source' -> 'producer' via $ref; add 'source' via $ref; remove base_seed; add top-level 'examples' with one coherent seed instance; keep additionalProperties:false.
task-schema.json (v2): remove 'prd' from properties and required; add required 'producer' and 'source' via $ref; add top-level 'examples' with one coherent task-file instance.
validate-schema.sh: build a referencing.Registry from all contracts/*.json keyed by bare $id; add examples[*] self-validation meta-check; add explicit schema_version-mismatch messaging.
register-skill/SKILL.md: add a codified step requiring a new lineage producer to append itself to the provenance-schema producer enum.

Producers to stamp producer/source: to-tasks, debug, code-review, improve-component, to-e2e-tasks, prototype, to-seed. Consumers to update: to-e2e-tasks (read source.ref), tasks-to-linear (slug from task filename).

to-seed/SKILL.md: repoint synthesis at contracts/seed-schema.json for shape and at its embedded example for pattern; change Mode-2 merge to set source={kind:'seed', ref:<parent>} instead of base_seed.
Delete skills/to-seed/resources/seed-example.json and resources/task-schema.md; repoint to-tasks line 75 at contracts/task-schema.json.
testing_decisions: After the validator upgrade, run validate-schema.sh against each embedded example to confirm examples[*] self-validation passes.
Confirm a cross-file $ref in seed-schema and task-schema resolves through the registry (a fixture instance validates clean).
Confirm an old-version seed/task file now fails with the explicit schema_version-mismatch message, not a generic property error.

out_of_scope.rationale: It is an ephemeral verdict, not a persisted lineage document.

out_of_scope.text: Migration script or dual-version validator acceptance
out_of_scope.rationale: Hard cutover only; old on-disk files fail by design.
Deferred until a real producer derives from a task file.

risks_and_tradeoffs: The validator upgrade touches a script 11 skills depend on; a wrong registry setup breaks validation everywhere, so it must be implemented and tested carefully.
Closed enums plus enforcement mean forgetting to register a new producer fails validation loudly; this coupling is intended (it forces registration) but is a sharp edge.
Hard cutover breaks any old-format files; accepted because seeds/tasks are disposable scaffolding and nothing is in flight.
further_notes: JSON Schema's 'examples' keyword is annotation-only and is not validated by default; the validator meta-check is what makes the embedded examples trustworthy.
Reverse-tracing the lineage is a uniform traversal: follow source.ref upward, switching on source.kind, until source is null (chain root).
